content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Accepted all three amendments. The first two identify concrete defects in the new propose-new-bead reconciliation path, and the third identifies an unsafe fallback that can journal the wrong bead id if create-output parsing ever stops being authoritative.

## Votes

- `fr-2-984dd490`: Accept
  `reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success` replays accepted `ProposeNewBead` amendments on every rerun, but `handle_propose_new_bead` has no journal-based dedupe. After one successful creation, a replay will hit the title match at `service.rs:2876` and reclassify the same finding as planned-elsewhere instead of recognizing the prior `ProposedBeadCreated` record. That breaks idempotency and corrupts the audit trail on exactly the crash/replay path this reconciliation code is meant to survive.
- `fr-2-cc4143e4`: Accept
  The handler creates the bead, resolves its id, flushes, and records a journal event, but never adds any dependency edge. There is no `add_dependency` call or equivalent placement logic in `service.rs:2921-2983`, so the follow-up bead is disconnected from the canonical bead graph. Given this flow is supposed to create routed follow-up work rather than an orphan issue, that is a real correctness/maintainability gap.
- `fr-2-cef2d3fb`: Accept
  If stdout parsing does not yield a usable id, `resolve_created_bead_id` falls back to `list_matching_bead_by_title`, which returns the first normalized title match without applying `eligible_existing_proposed_bead_match`. That means the fallback can select a closed duplicate or even the active bead itself, despite those cases being explicitly excluded during pre-check. Writing that incorrect id into the journal would permanently misidentify the created follow-up bead.
